paragraphs.text: Jacob and all his house intended to go into Egypt. But since Jacob feared that Egyptian sorceries might turn to harm against his sons, God appeared to him and said: “Fear not to go down into Egypt” (Gen 46:3). And since Jacob also thought that, by reason of the abundance of Egyptian goods, his sons would remain forever in Egypt, and thus the promise would be left unfulfilled, God says further: “I will go down with thee… and I will surely bring thee up again.” Finally, since Jacob feared that Joseph would soon die, God says: “And Joseph thy son shall put his hand upon thine eyes” (Gen 46:4). After this Jacob arose and with joy went down to Egypt with seventy souls, including Joseph and his two sons. Joseph went out to meet his father with chariots and a multitude of people. And meeting Jacob, he bowed himself to him, and they wept on each other’s neck.
